title: Document Topic Modeling
tagline: Discover themes in support ticket corpora

sections.problem: Support wanted a high-level view of what customers were asking about without reading thousands of tickets. Manual tagging was inconsistent and didn't scale.
sections.data: A synthetic corpus of 5k support tickets (short paragraphs). I preprocessed: lowercasing, stopwords, lemmatization, and built a dictionary and bag-of-words corpus. Vocabulary was trimmed to terms that appeared in at least 5 documents and in at most 50% of documents.
sections.approach: I ran LDA with 6 topics and tuned the number of topics using coherence (C_v). I compared with NMF on TF-IDF. Best coherence was at k=6; I labeled topics by top terms and sampled documents per topic for sanity checks.
sections.results: LDA with 6 topics gave coherence 0.42. Top topics aligned with billing, login issues, feature requests, and API errors. The chart shows the top terms per topic for two example topics.
next: Add a temporal dimension to track topic drift.
Use topic mixtures to route tickets automatically.
Experiment with BERT-based embeddings for short texts.

topic_terms.title: Top terms for two example topics
